column1262: the patient receiving ongoing hormonal therapy for breast cancer must have been on the same hormonal agent for at least 3 months prior to study enrollment and plan to continue for the duration of the study (9 months)
column1263: the patient may use psychotropic medications (anti depressants, anxiolytics, sleeping aids, narcotics) if they have been on the medication for at least 12 weeks and the dose has been stable for at least 4 weeks prior to enrollment
column1264: the patient who was previously on a psychotropic medication and has discontinued the drug must have been off the medication for at least 4 weeks prior to enrollment
column1265: the patient who has been on a psychotropic medication for at least 12 weeks but has recently switched to a medicine in the same class must be on a stable dose of the new medication for at least 4 weeks prior to enrollment
column1266: the patient has a self reported cognitive problem plus a measured memory deficit, defined as a score of 7 or less on a single trial of eligibility pre screen hvlt r form 3
column1267: the patient is able to understand and willing to sign a written informed consent document
column1268: the patient is able to speak english
column1269: the patient currently taking a moderate risk qtc prolongation medication is eligible if the medication is stopped and the patient is off the medication for at least 5 half lives before starting the study drug
column1270: the patient currently taking a moderate risk qtc prolongation medication is eligible if they continue the medication and have a normal qtc interval (≤ 460 milliseconds) on a screening ecg following informed consent and prior to study enrollment, and the qtc level must be ≤ 500 milliseconds at designated follow up visits
column1271: the patient currently taking a moderate risk qtc prolongation medication that is only taken occasionally may be eligible if the medication is stopped at the discretion of the treating site physician and the patient is off the medication for at least 5 half lives prior to starting the study drug
column1272: the patient currently taking a moderate risk bradycardia causing agent is eligible if the medication is stopped and the patient is off the medication for at least 5 half lives before starting the study drug
column1273: the patient currently taking a moderate risk bradycardia causing agent is eligible if they continue the medication and have a resting heart rate of at least 55 beats per minute at screening following informed consent, with monitoring at specified time points
column1274: the patient currently taking a moderate risk bradycardia causing agent that is only taken occasionally may be eligible if the medication is stopped at the discretion of the treating site physician and the patient is off the medication for at least 5 half lives prior to starting the study drug
column1275: the patient is female with histologically diagnosed relapsed high grade serous ovarian cancer, including primary peritoneal and/or fallopian tube cancer, or high grade endometrioid cancer
column1276: the patient is eligible to undergo brca testing even if recurrence or progression of disease has not yet occurred more than 6 months (at least 183 days) after completion of their last platinum therapy
column1277: the patient has a documented germline mutation in brca1 and/or brca2 that is predicted to be deleterious or suspected deleterious
column1278: the patient has at least one lesion (measurable and/or non-measurable) that can be accurately assessed at baseline by ct or mri and is suitable for repeated assessment
column1279: the patient has received at least two prior platinum based lines of chemotherapy for ovarian cancer
column1280: the patient is partially platinum sensitive, defined as progression 6 to 12 months after the end of the last platinum based chemotherapy, or platinum sensitive, defined as progression more than 12 months after the end of the last platinum based chemotherapy
column1281: the patient is suitable to start treatment with single agent chemotherapy based on physician’s choice of weekly paclitaxel, topotecan, pegylated liposomal doxorubicin (pld), or gemcitabine
column1282: the patient has normal organ and bone marrow function measured within 28 days of randomisation, including haemoglobin at least 10.0 g/dl with no packed red cells transfusions in the past 28 days
column1283: the patient has a total bilirubin level less than or equal to 1.5 times the institutional upper limit of normal
column1284: the patient has aspartate aminotransferase (ast) and alanine aminotransferase (alt) levels less than or equal to 2.5 times the institutional upper limit of normal, unless liver metastases are present, in which case they must be less than or equal to 5 times the upper limit of normal
column1285: the patient has a serum creatinine level less than or equal to 1.5 times the institutional upper limit of normal
column1286: the patient has an eastern cooperative oncology group (ecog) performance status of 0 to 2
column1287: the patient shows evidence of non childbearing status, defined as a negative urine or serum pregnancy test within 7 days before first study drug dose for women of childbearing potential, or is postmenopausal
column1288: the patient is postmenopausal, defined as age 60 years or older, or age under 60 with amenorrhea for 1 year or more in the absence of chemotherapy and/or hormonal treatments, or with luteinizing hormone (lh), follicle stimulating hormone (fsh), and oestradiol levels in the postmenopausal range, or with radiation induced oophorectomy with last menses more than 1 year ago, or with chemotherapy induced menopause with more than 1 year interval since last menses, or with surgical sterilisation (bilateral oophorectomy or hysterectomy)
column1289: the patient is willing and able to comply with the protocol for the duration of the study, including undergoing treatment and scheduled visits and examinations
column1290: the patient has a formalin fixed, paraffin embedded (ffpe) tumour sample from the primary or recurrent cancer available for central testing
column1291: the patient has read, understood, and signed the written informed consent before any study-related activity, agreeing to participate in the study and to comply with study requirements
column1292: the patient is naïve to moderately or highly emetogenic antineoplastic agents
column1293: the patient is scheduled to receive at least 4 consecutive cycles of an ac combination regimen
column1294: the patient is either of non-childbearing potential or of childbearing potential using reliable contraceptive measures and having a negative urine pregnancy test within 24 hours prior to dosing of investigational product
column1295: the patient of non-childbearing potential is defined as being in post-menopausal state for at least 1 year, or having documented surgical sterilization or hysterectomy at least 3 months before study participation
column1296: the patient uses reliable contraceptive measures, which include implants, injectables, combined oral contraceptives, intrauterine devices, vasectomized partner, or complete (long term) sexual abstinence
column1297: the patient has adequate hematologic and metabolic status for receiving a cycle of ac chemotherapy based on the investigator's assessment
column1298: the patient with known hepatic or renal impairment may be enrolled at the discretion of the investigator
column1299: the patient is able to read, understand, follow the study procedure, and complete the patient diary
column1300: the patient’s participation in the next cycle of chemotherapy is considered appropriate by the investigator and does not pose unwarranted risk
column1301: the patient is scheduled to receive an ac chemotherapy regimen or ac chemotherapy together with other chemotherapies as defined in the protocol for cycle 1
column1302: the patient’s adequate hematologic and metabolic status for receiving a cycle of ac chemotherapy is confirmed according to the investigator's opinion
